title: Textiles, garments and related trades n.e.c.
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Design and develop manufactured products, such as cars, home appliances, and children's toys. Combine artistic talent with research on product use, marketing, and materials to create the most functional and appealing product design. Design clothing and accessories. Create original designs or adapt fashion trends. (Based on 24 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Confer with clients regarding price, type of arrangement desired, and delivery details.
Guide and position garments or garment parts under machine needles and presser feet to sew parts together, monitor machine operation, and adjust settings as needed for correct stitching.

Prepare materials for sewing by placing spools of thread, inserting bobbins, threading machine guides, and ensuring materials match the specified design, including marking and cutting patterns accordingly.
Inspect and verify the quality of completed garments or articles by examining, weighing, and measuring them against design specifications, addressing any defects found during inspection.
Repair or alter broken or defective garments and fabrics by adding parts, restitching, or making alterations for improved fit.
Develop and create garment patterns and designs by drawing them by hand or using computers, ensuring they are correctly sized and suitable for the intended materials and styles.
Select materials, threads, fasteners, and accessories according to job requirements, and order necessary materials and supplies from wholesalers or retailers.
Fabricate samples or mockups of garments using various materials, ensuring designs are functional and aesthetically appealing according to client specifications.
Supervise and coordinate workers in the sewing process, ensuring quality production, and assist with training and instruction as necessary.
Communicate with clients about design specifications and incorporate feedback into the final product designs or arrangements, ensuring satisfaction with the outcome.
Perform maintenance on sewing machines and other equipment to ensure proper functionality and prevent malfunctions during production.
Create and manage in-store displays, decorations, and overall shop presentation to enhance the customer experience and promote products effectively.
Deliver completed garments and supervise tasks related to customer orders, ensuring timely and accurate fulfillment of client requests.
Market and promote produced garments and designs through various channels, including fashion shows and retail presentations, to reach the target audience effectively.

Examine tags on garments to determine alterations that are needed, confer with customers to determine types of material and garment styles desired, and measure customers using tape measures, recording measurements.

Create design specifications to provide instructions on garment sewing and assembly, and develop, copy, or adapt designs for garments with patterns that fit measurements, applying knowledge of garment design, construction, styling, and fabric.
Maintain garment drape and proportions as alterations are performed, including making garment style changes such as tapering pant legs, narrowing lapels, and adding or removing padding.
Press garments using hand irons or pressing machines, and put in padding and shaping materials.
Sew buttonholes, attach buttons, and sew, join, reinforce, or finish parts of articles such as garments, using needles and thread or other materials.
Position patterns of garment parts on fabric and cut the fabric along outlines using scissors, and trim excess material using scissors or knives.
Repair or replace defective garment parts such as pockets, zippers, snaps, buttons, and linings.

Estimate how much a garment will cost to make based on factors such as time and material requirements, and research production specifications, costs, production materials, and manufacturing methods to provide cost estimates and itemized production requirements.
Test patterns by making and fitting sample garments, and prepare sketches of ideas, detailed drawings or blueprints, using drafting instruments or computer-aided design equipment.

Present designs and reports to customers for approval and discuss the need for modifications, and evaluate the feasibility of design ideas based on appearance, safety, function, serviceability, budget, and market characteristics.
Modify and refine designs using working models to conform with customer specifications, production limitations, or changes in design trends.
Inspect products to ensure specifications are met and determine if machines need adjustment, including monitoring inventory levels on a regular basis, ordering additional supplies as necessary.
Inspect machinery to determine whether repairs are needed, and notify supervisors or repair staff of mechanical malfunctions.
Operate machines for test runs to verify adjustments and obtain product samples, start machines, monitor operations, and make adjustments as needed.
Thread yarn, thread, or fabric through guides, needles, and rollers of machines for weaving, knitting, or other processing.
Clean and maintain tools and equipment using solvents, brushes, and rags, and inspect products to verify that they meet specifications and determine if machine adjustment is needed.
Sew garments using different sewing techniques such as felling, tacking, basting, embroidery, and fagoting.
Trim excess threads or edges of parts and clean, treat, buff, or polish finished items using grinders or cleaners, as necessary.
Maintain time or production records, and read job orders to inspect workpieces to determine work procedures and materials required.

Identify equipment options for machine setups, such as compressors and handling cutting and assembly equipment for textile processing, including setting up or operating auxiliary equipment involved in cutting or sewing.
Inspect articles for defects, remove damaged or worn parts, and evaluate quality, using hand tools and calibrated equipment.
Create functional or decorative objects by hand, using a variety of methods and materials. Design clothing and accessories, including creating original designs or adapting fashion trends.
Cut, shape, fit, join, mold, or otherwise process materials, such as fabric, fleece, leather, or wood, using hand tools, power tools, or machinery.
Select materials for use based on strength, color, texture, balance, weight, size, malleability, and other characteristics, and prepare them for crafting.
Confer with customers to assess needs or obtain feedback on product designs or modifications.
Plan and attend craft shows to market products and develop marketing strategies, including advertising and pricing strategies for products.
Create prototypes or models of objects to be crafted, and develop product concepts or creative ideas.
Pack products for shipping and ensure proper display for sale or exhibition.
Research craft trends, venues, and customer buying patterns to inspire product designs and marketing strategies.
Adjust machines and equipment for production processes and maintain operations data, such as production records.
Conduct employee training in equipment operations or work and safety procedures, or assign training tasks to experienced workers.
Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions, ensuring compliance with production standards.
Plan and develop new products to meet production goals based on specifications and customer needs.
Evaluate employee performance and enforce safety regulations within production environments.
Direct and coordinate the activities of employees engaged in the production or processing of goods, ensuring smooth operation between departments.
Estimate costs for requested products or services, such as custom footwear or repairs, and track customer payments.
